title: Novavax Announces Sale of Czech Manufacturing Facility to Novo Nordisk for $200 Million
text: Novavax has signed a definitive agreement to sell its manufacturing facility in Bohumil, Czech Republic to Novo Nordisk for $200 million. The facility divestment is expected to slash annual operating costs by around $80 million. The 150,000-square-foot recombinant protein facility in Bohumil is being sold to Novo Nordisk. Novo will receive the production site, support buildings and the plant's entire existing workforce, which stands at around 300 people. Novavax will receive $190 million from the sale in cash this year, with another $10 million to come in 2025. The decision aligns with Novavax's previously announced commitment to evolve into a more lean and agile organization focused on partnering its pipeline assets and technology platform. The sale will help Novavax focus on its early- and late-stage pipeline of protein-based vaccines leveraging the company's bespoke Matrix-M adjuvant. Novo Nordisk confirmed the Czech plant will not manufacture its blockbuster GLP-1 drug products Ozempic and Wegovy, and will instead provide mammalian production capacity for existing and future products. This strategic asset sale reflects Novavax's pivot away from manufacturing-heavy operations toward a more asset-light partnership model.
